plans.name: Pro
plans.details: Claude Code terminal access, file creation, code execution, unlimited projects. Includes Claude Code in terminal + desktop app.

plans.name: Max (5x)
plans.details: All Pro features + 5x more usage compared to Pro. For power users.

plans.name: Max (20x)
plans.details: All Pro features + 20x more usage compared to Pro. For heavy power users.

plans.name: Team Standard
plans.details: Core collaboration: chat, projects, more usage. Does NOT include Claude Code.

plans.name: Team Premium
plans.details: All Team features + Claude Code access + early access to new collaboration features.

plans.name: Enterprise
plans.details: Custom pricing. All Team Premium features + governance, security, SSO, custom workflows.

key_features: Agentic CLI tool that reads, edits files, and runs terminal commands autonomously
Full codebase comprehension across multiple files and directories
Native VS Code extension (also works in Cursor, Windsurf)
JetBrains IDEs support (IntelliJ, PyCharm, WebStorm)
Multi-agent orchestration: spawns sub-agents for parallel task execution
Model Context Protocol (MCP) for connecting to external tools (Jira, Slack, Google Drive)
Git workflow automation: creates pull requests, manages branches
Desktop app for visual diff review and multi-session management
Cloud sessions support for long-running background tasks
Agent SDK for building custom agents
Web browser access in addition to CLI and IDE
tools.benchmark_notes: Claude Opus 4.6 model achieves 80.8% on SWE-bench verified. Some sources report 74.4% for a specific evaluation configuration.

adoption_stats.growth_period: Reached #1 in ~8 months after May 2025 launch

notable_strengths: Highest SWE-bench score (80.8%) among mainstream tools
Best-in-class agentic capabilities for complex multi-file tasks
MCP ecosystem for deep integrations with enterprise tools
Multi-agent parallelism for large codebase tasks
Fastest growing AI coding tool in history (8 months to #1)
Strong model quality: Opus 4.6 leads coding benchmarks
Works across terminal, IDE extensions, desktop app, and browser
notable_weaknesses: No free tier available
Primarily CLI-first — less visual than IDE-native tools
Subscription includes Anthropic usage limits (not unlimited raw compute)
Relatively newer tool ecosystem compared to Copilot
Can be expensive for heavy enterprise usage at scale
best_for: Developers tackling complex, multi-file architectural tasks
Teams wanting maximum agentic autonomy
Power users willing to pay for best-in-class model quality
Startups and individual developers who want the best results
MCP-based integrations with enterprise toolchains

plans.name: Hobby (Free)
plans.details: Limited Agent requests and Tab completions per month. No credit card required.

plans.name: Pro
plans.details: 500 fast premium model requests/month + credit pool equal to plan price. Unlimited standard completions. Access to GPT-5.4, Claude Opus 4.6, Gemini 3 Pro, Grok Code.

plans.name: Pro+
plans.details: Everything in Pro with 3x the usage credits ($60 credit pool). Same model access.

plans.name: Ultra
plans.details: 20x the usage of Pro plan. Priority access to new features. For heavy power users.

plans.name: Teams
plans.details: Per user. All Pro features + centralized billing, SSO, admin controls.

plans.name: Enterprise
plans.details: Custom pricing. Everything in Teams + pooled usage, invoice billing, dedicated support.

key_features: VS Code fork — full VS Code extension compatibility
Composer/Agent Mode: multi-file editing across 10-100+ files simultaneously
Multi-model support: GPT-5.4, Claude Opus 4.6, Gemini 3 Pro, Grok Code on same plan
Custom embedding-based codebase indexing for best-in-class recall
Tab autocomplete (Cursor-native, fast inline completions)
Credit-based system (switched from request-based in June 2025)
JetBrains support via Agent Client Protocol (ACP)
Custom model training on team codebase
Privacy mode: code never stored for training
Auto mode for unlimited standard usage
ide_support: JetBrains IDEs via ACP (IntelliJ, PyCharm, WebStorm)
All VS Code extensions work natively
tools.benchmark_notes: Cursor (as a tool/system) scores ~51.7% on SWE-bench. Underlying models (Claude Opus 4.6, GPT-5.4) score higher individually.

adoption_stats.speed_advantage: 30% faster task completion vs Copilot on SWE-bench (62.95s vs 89.91s)

notable_strengths: Most powerful multi-file editing via Composer Mode
Access to ALL frontier models on a single subscription
Familiar VS Code experience with AI deeply embedded
Custom codebase indexing for superior context recall
40% faster feature shipping than VS Code + Copilot setup
Flexible credit model adapts to usage patterns
Strong community and extension ecosystem
notable_weaknesses: Credit-based billing can be confusing and unpredictable
Requires switching from existing IDE to Cursor
Not a standalone model — relies on third-party models (OpenAI, Anthropic)
Enterprise features less mature than GitHub Copilot
Auto mode model quality varies (Cursor picks cheapest model)
best_for: Developers wanting an all-in-one AI-native IDE
Teams needing multi-model flexibility on one plan
Complex architectural refactoring across many files
VS Code users wanting to upgrade to AI-first experience
Developers who want to use the best model for each task

plans.name: Free
plans.details: 2,000 completions/month + 50 chat requests. Limited features. No credit card required.

plans.name: Pro
plans.details: Unlimited completions, full chat, agent mode, CLI support. Access to GPT-5.4, Claude Sonnet 4.6, Gemini 2.5 Pro.

plans.name: Pro+
plans.details: Everything in Pro + higher premium request allowances. $0.04/request overage.

plans.name: Business
plans.details: Per user. All Pro features + audit logs, policy enforcement, no code training opt-out.

plans.name: Enterprise
plans.details: Per user. GitHub Enterprise Cloud required. All Business features + codebase indexing, knowledge bases, fine-tuning.

plans.name: Student
plans.details: Free for verified students. Full premium features.

key_features: Inline code completions (fastest and most accurate in market)
Copilot Chat (conversational coding assistance in IDE)
Agent Mode (autonomous multi-step task execution)
Copilot CLI (terminal-native development assistance)
Cloud-based Coding Agent (assigned via GitHub Issues, produces PRs autonomously)
Multi-model support: GPT-5.4, Claude Sonnet 4.6, Gemini 2.5 Pro
Deep GitHub integration: PRs, issues, code review
Enterprise: SOC 2 Type II, ISO 27001, SSO, audit logs, IP indemnity
Codebase indexing respects .gitignore (sensitive file exclusion)
Custom instructions and knowledge bases
tools.benchmark_notes: GitHub Copilot scores 56.0% on SWE-bench tasks. Completions are faster than Cursor (89.91s average per task) but accuracy is higher than Cursor's 51.7%.

adoption_stats.most_loved_rating: 9% 'most loved' among developers (down from historical highs)
adoption_stats.pr_time_reduction: From 9.6 days to 2.4 days average PR turnaround in enterprise
adoption_stats.user_count: Over 1.8 million paid subscribers (as of 2025 data)

notable_strengths: Best enterprise governance, security, and compliance (SOC 2, ISO 27001)
Widest IDE support in the industry (10+ editors)
Deepest GitHub platform integration — PRs, issues, reviews, Actions
Fastest and most accurate inline completions
Deployed at 90% of Fortune 100 — battle-tested at scale
Multi-model support within single subscription
Cloud-based coding agent for fully autonomous PR generation
Lowest entry price for paid tier ($10/month)
notable_weaknesses: Weakest on complex multi-file agentic tasks (10+ files)
Premium request metering is confusing and complaint-generating
Lower 'most loved' rating than Cursor or Claude Code
Agent mode less capable than Claude Code or Cursor Composer for architectural tasks
Enterprise lock-in requires GitHub Enterprise Cloud for top tier
best_for: Enterprise teams already on GitHub/Microsoft ecosystem
Developers wanting the best inline completion experience
Organizations requiring audit logs, SSO, and compliance
Budget-conscious developers (best value at $10/month)
Teams needing widest IDE coverage across the organization
Students (free full-featured access)

plans.name: Free
plans.details: 25 credits/month. Unlimited SWE-1 Lite usage. 1 deploy/day. Unlimited Tab autocomplete. Good for experiments or BYOK.

plans.name: Pro
plans.details: 500 credits/month (~$20 value). SWE-1 model access. 5 deploys/day. Best for solo developers.

plans.name: Teams
plans.details: Per user. 500 credits per user + admin tools, billing controls, priority support.

plans.name: Enterprise
plans.details: Custom pricing. Everything in Teams + RBAC, SSO, hybrid deployment. 200+ seats doubles to 1000 credits/user.

key_features: Cascade: proprietary agentic AI engine for full codebase understanding
SWE-1 and SWE-1.5 proprietary models (13x faster than Sonnet 4.5 claimed)
Cascade Memories: remembers coding patterns, project structure across sessions
Parallel agent sessions (Wave 13, early 2026): multiple Cascade instances simultaneously
Plan Mode: detailed implementation planning before code generation
Unlimited Tab autocomplete on all plans including Free
Multi-file agentic editing with terminal command execution
Built-in deployment capabilities (1-5/day depending on plan)
Credit-based system where Tab never consumes credits
tools.benchmark_notes: Windsurf's SWE-1.5 model claims 13x faster inference than Sonnet 4.5. Ranked #1 in LogRocket AI Dev Tool Power Rankings (February 2026). Specific SWE-bench % not publicly disclosed for the overall tool.

adoption_stats.note: Strong in the beginner and solo developer segment

notable_strengths: Best value for money at $15/month Pro tier
Cascade's memory system for persistent project context
Proprietary SWE-1.5 model optimized for speed
Unlimited Tab autocomplete never consumes credits
Parallel agent sessions for complex parallel workloads
Strong agentic multi-file editing at competitive price
LogRocket #1 AI dev tool ranking (Feb 2026)
notable_weaknesses: Smaller model ecosystem than Cursor (mostly proprietary SWE models)
Less mature enterprise features vs GitHub Copilot
Credit system complexity (different from older quota model)
Less brand recognition and community than Cursor or Copilot
SWE-bench score not publicly disclosed for independent verification
best_for: Solo developers wanting best value agentic IDE
Beginners looking for the most approachable AI IDE
Teams wanting parallel agent workflows
Developers prioritizing speed of AI inference
Budget-conscious teams wanting strong agentic features

plans.name: Code Assistant

plans.name: Agentic Platform
plans.details: Per user. Full agentic capabilities + enterprise-grade security. Average G2 figure.

Air-gapped deployment option (fully offline)

SOC 2, GDPR, ISO 27001 compliance

Code completions, chat, and agentic workflows

Works completely offline in air-gapped environments

best_for: Regulated industries (finance, healthcare, government)

plans.name: Open Source (Free)
plans.details: Apache 2.0 license. Full features free. BYOK (Bring Your Own Key) for any model provider.

plans.name: Paid/Enterprise
plans.details: Optional paid features and enterprise support available. Contact for pricing.

BYOK: connect any model (OpenAI, Claude, Gemini, local Llama via Ollama)
Chat, Autocomplete, Edit, and Agent Mode
Self-hostable: full privacy, code never leaves your machine
VS Code and JetBrains native extensions

Quality control checks enforceable in CI
Model-agnostic: switch between providers seamlessly
Recommended: Codestral (Mistral) for autocomplete, Claude/GPT for chat
tools.benchmark_notes: No proprietary model — benchmark performance depends on the underlying model chosen by the user.

recommendations_by_use_case.reason: Highest SWE-bench score (80.8%), best agentic capabilities, MCP integrations, #1 developer adoption in 2026. Worth the $20/month for serious developers.

recommendations_by_use_case.use_case: Best AI-native IDE with multi-model flexibility
recommendations_by_use_case.reason: Access to ALL frontier models (GPT-5.4, Claude Opus 4.6, Gemini 3 Pro) on one $20/month plan. Composer Mode excels at 10-100 file edits. Familiar VS Code experience.
Widest IDE support, strongest enterprise governance (SOC 2, ISO 27001, IP indemnity), deployed at 90% of Fortune 100, deepest GitHub platform integration.

recommendations_by_use_case.use_case: Best budget option with agentic features
recommendations_by_use_case.reason: Strong agentic capabilities at $15/month (vs $20 for Cursor/Claude Code). Unlimited Tab autocomplete even on free plan. #1 in LogRocket rankings Feb 2026.

recommendations_by_use_case.use_case: Best for regulated industries / air-gapped environments
recommendations_by_use_case.reason: Only tool offering true air-gapped deployment with zero code retention. SOC 2, GDPR, ISO 27001 certified. Essential for finance, healthcare, defense sectors.
Apache 2.0 open source, completely free with BYOK. Maximum model flexibility, full privacy with local models via Ollama. No vendor lock-in.
Free plan for verified students with full premium features. Lowest paid barrier at $10/month. Widest tutorial and community resources.

recommendations_by_use_case.use_case: Best for startups and solo developers
recommendations_by_use_case.reason: 75% of startups use Claude Code. Best results for complex tasks. $20/month Pro plan provides access to the strongest coding model available.

recommendations_by_use_case.use_case: Best for teams wanting parallel agent workflows
recommendations_by_use_case.reason: Wave 13 introduced parallel Cascade sessions — multiple agents working on different codebase areas simultaneously. Unique feature at competitive price.

recommendations_by_use_case.use_case: Best for developers already deep in GitHub ecosystem
recommendations_by_use_case.reason: Native GitHub issues-to-PR automation, Codespaces integration, Actions-based cloud agent. No workflow disruption for existing GitHub users.

market_context.overall_adoption_2026: 73% of engineering teams use AI coding tools daily (up from 41% in 2025)
market_context.ai_generated_code_pct: 41-51% of all code committed to GitHub is AI-generated or AI-assisted
market_context.productivity_gain: ~3.6 hours/week saved per developer on average
market_context.developer_survey_notes: Claude Code overtook GitHub Copilot and Cursor in 8 months; 95% weekly AI coding tool usage among surveyed developers in 2026

sources.title: Claude Code vs Cursor vs GitHub Copilot: 2026 Showdown
LogRocket AI Dev Tool Power Rankings (March 2026)
AI Tooling for Software Engineers 2026 (Pragmatic Engineer)

data_quality_notes: Pricing verified from official sources for all 7 tools. SWE-bench scores available for Claude Code (80.8%), GitHub Copilot (~56%), and Cursor (~51.7%). Windsurf, Tabnine, and Continue do not publish SWE-bench scores. Adoption stats from multiple independent surveys corroborate Claude Code's #1 position. Minor uncertainty on exact Cursor and Copilot SWE-bench percentages as different evaluation configurations yield different results (51.7% vs higher depending on methodology).
